At the Augustana College Local Agriculture Club food forest/orchard Falling Fruit season: August-September.
Apple Tree on an abandoned lot that is now owned by the city of Rock Island, Illinois. No one cares if you pick from it. Falling Fruit season: September-October.

A grafted Apple tree, one half is small berry- like crabapples, but the other half are small, sweet apples similar to a Wilson. Big enough to eat or peel. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: July-September.

Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
Pear Tree on abandoned lot now owned by the City of Rock Island, Illinois. No one cares if you pick the fruit. Falling Fruit season: September-October.
